title: Differences Between Static and Dynamic Server Pages
breadcrumbs: Differences Between Static and Dynamic Server Pages
text: Differences Between Static and Dynamic Server Pages The following table describes the differences between Static and Dynamic Server Pages . Table 1. Differences Between Static and Dynamic Server Pages Issue DSP USP Notes Component type Dynamic Server Pages Static Server Pages None. Page Updating Both full page and incremental Full page None. Communication Asynchronous, using Ajax Synchronous, using form submit None. Layout Well-formatted XHTML Proprietary XHTML For both DSPs and USPs, the layout can be maintained with the internal editor or any XHTML-compliant editor. It is also possible to store the layout in files per web page. Binding Standard XHTML tags using id attribute Proprietary tags using XHTML name attribute Derived entities and fields must be bound to elements in the server page. For more details, see Binding Web Layout to Component Structure . Widgets Rich set of Javascript-based widgets Limited set of controls For more details, see Widgets for Dynamic Server Pages and Widgets for Static Server Pages . Dynamic widget properties Supported Not supported None. Interactive Triggers All widgets have an OnChange extended trigger, and some have a detail trigger No interactive triggers, except detail for Button control. None. Syntax checking Done in the browser using JavaScript Not supported For more details, see Syntax Checking in Web Applications . JavaScript Requires JavaScript JavaScript not required, although it can be used JavaScript is used in USPs for browser-side field syntax checking, but it is possible to run a USP without this. ProcScript Web and DSP-specific ProcScript Web-specific ProcScript For more details, see ProcScript for Dynamic Server Pages and ProcScript for Static Server Pages . Error handling Must be programmed Must be programmed For more details, see Data Error Handling in Web Applications . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
